title: ThreadStatic fields should not be initialized
description: "ThreadStatic" fields should not be initializedAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearreliabilityBugmulti-threadingWhy is this an issue?When an object has a field annotated withThreadStatic, that field is shared within a given thread, but unique across threads. Since a class' static initializer is only invoked for the first thread created, it also means that only the first thread will have the expected initial values.Instead, allow such fields to be initialized to their default values or make the initialization lazy.Noncompliant code examplepublic class Foo { [ThreadStatic] public static object PerThreadObject = new object(); // Noncompliant. Will be null in all the threads except the first one.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: ThreadStatic fields should not be initialized inline - only the first thread will have the expected value. Use lazy initialization instead.

logic.triggering_conditions: source contains [ThreadStatic] attribute with inline field initialization (= value)
logic.reasoning: When a field is annotated with [ThreadStatic], it becomes thread-local. However, static initializers only run for the first thread, so subsequent threads will get default values (null, 0, etc.) instead of the initialized value. Lazy initialization should be used instead.

should_trigger: [ThreadStatic] public static object PerThreadObject = new object();
